instincts.title: Use uv run for Python commands
instincts.trigger: When about to run any Python command (tests, linters, scripts, REPL)
instincts.action: Use `uv run ...` (never `python`, `pip`, or bare tool binaries). Prefer `uv run pytest`, `uv run ruff check .`, etc.

instincts.title: Address LSP diagnostics early
instincts.trigger: After editing code or before declaring work complete
instincts.action: Run LSP diagnostics on touched files and fix errors/warnings before lint/tests; don’t defer LSP cleanup.

evidence.note: Team core edits are high-churn and benefit from catching type/ordering issues early; reinforces running LSP diagnostics on touched files before lint/tests.
Project guidance says: do not use lsp_diagnostics; use `uv run basedpyright` instead.

instincts.title: Prompt changes require prompt tests
instincts.trigger: When editing agent prompts or prompt assembly code
instincts.action: Update/add focused tests covering the prompt contract and run the prompt test suite.

evidence.note: Recent work touched src/agent_loom/team/prompts.py and updated tests/test_team_prompts.py, reinforcing that prompt edits should be paired with prompt contract tests.
prompts.py and tests/test_team_prompts.py both changed in this session; reinforces treating prompt output as a deterministic contract.
Team prompt changes landed with adjacent behavior changes; prompt outputs should be treated like APIs and covered by focused tests.
Team prompt-facing behavior shifted with corresponding test adjustments (tests/test_team_prompts.py), supporting the rule that prompt contract changes require prompt-focused tests.

Prompt-facing behavior change (team prompts) was validated via targeted prompt tests, matching the intended gate of updating tests/test_team_prompts.py alongside prompt changes.

instincts.title: Autolearn output must be CompoundSpec v2 JSON only
instincts.trigger: When responding to a background autolearn prompt
instincts.action: Output only valid JSON matching CompoundSpec v2; no commentary, no code fences, no product-code edits.

evidence.note: Background autolearn prompt reiterates JSON-only requirement; reinforcing this reduces tool-application failures.
This session included a Background Autolearn Prompt requiring strict JSON-only CompoundSpec v2 output; reinforcing the discipline prevents tool-apply failures.
Autolearn prompt reiterated strict JSON-only output requirement; session included a system reminder forbidding edits during plan mode.

instincts.title: Team core changes require targeted tests
instincts.trigger: When editing src/agent_loom/team/core.py, src/agent_loom/team/prompts.py, or src/agent_loom/team/cli.py
instincts.action: Update/add focused tests covering the changed behavior (especially prompt contracts) and run `uv run pytest` for the relevant test module(s) plus `uv run ruff check .` before calling the work done.

evidence.note: Large edits across src/agent_loom/team/core.py and new/expanded cli/inbox modules suggest keeping targeted tests + ruff as a hard gate.

instincts.title: Team Expansion Requires Contract Tests + Ruff
instincts.trigger: When adding or significantly expanding team runtime (new CLI entrypoints, inbox loop, or new team modules like inbox/models/constants)
instincts.action: Treat it like a public API: add/adjust prompt-contract tests, run `uv run ruff check .`, run targeted `uv run pytest` for the changed surface area, and clear LSP diagnostics on touched files before calling work done.

evidence.note: Team expansion work landed with contract tests (CLI UX). Keep reinforcing the expectation: deterministic output + targeted tests + ruff via uv.
Team runtime expansion touched multiple team modules (core/cli/targets/models) and added/updated contract tests; reinforces the 'treat as public API + ruff + targeted pytest' workflow.
Team runtime expanded across core/inbox/models/targets with accompanying contract test coverage; reinforces treating this surface as API-like.
Team runtime expansion landed alongside a purpose-built contract test and should remain the default expectation for future expansions.

instincts.title: Ticket Core Changes Require Targeted Tests
instincts.trigger: When editing src/agent_loom/ticket/cli.py, src/agent_loom/ticket/core.py, or src/agent_loom/ticket/store.py
instincts.action: Treat it like a public API: run lsp_diagnostics on touched files, fix issues, run `uv run ruff check .`, and add/run targeted pytest coverage for ticket CLI/core/store behavior before declaring done.

evidence.note: Added/expanded ticket CLI+core behavior alongside new focused test module tests/test_ticket_ux.py; reinforces the need for targeted contract tests when touching src/agent_loom/ticket/*.

instincts.title: UI changes require LSP + ruff + targeted tests
instincts.trigger: After editing Python UI code under src/agent_loom/ui/ (for example src/agent_loom/ui/ticket_ui.py)
instincts.action: Run lsp_diagnostics on touched UI files, fix all findings, then run `uv run ruff check .` and the smallest relevant `uv run pytest ...` subset (or full suite if unsure).

instincts.action: Make output deterministic (explicit ordering, stable formatting) and add a focused pytest contract test for the rendered text.

instincts.title: Plan mode is read-only
instincts.trigger: System reminder says Plan Mode ACTIVE / READ-ONLY phase
instincts.action: Do not edit/create/delete files or run write-capable commands; only inspect/read/search and produce an execution plan or required JSON payload.

evidence.note: Plan Mode READ-ONLY reminder present; reinforces never editing or running write-capable commands under constraints.
System reminder explicitly enforces Plan Mode read-only with zero exceptions; reinforce as overriding constraint.
System reminder indicated Plan Mode (read-only) was active; reinforces avoiding any edits/tooling that would modify repo state.
System reminder reiterated Plan Mode is strict read-only: no edits or write-capable commands/tools.
Autolearn context included the explicit Plan Mode READ-ONLY system reminder with strict prohibition on edits and non-readonly tool usage; reinforces treating plan mode as a hard gate.
This session included an explicit system reminder that Plan Mode is active and forbids edits; reinforces keeping Plan Mode strictly read-only and using only inspect/read actions.
Plan Mode READ-ONLY system reminder appeared during this session; reinforces that any write/edit tool use is forbidden in this phase.
Plan-mode system reminder present; reinforces read-only behavior during restricted phases.
System reminder explicitly enforced Plan Mode read-only: no edits, no write-capable commands, inspection only.
Plan Mode system reminder reiterated read-only constraints (no edits/tooling beyond inspection) during this autolearn prompt.

instincts.title: Ticket file format changes require round-trip tests
instincts.trigger: When editing src/agent_loom/ticket/frontmatter.py or changing the ticket on-disk format/serialization
instincts.action: Add/adjust round-trip tests that load+save+reload tickets; ensure parse errors include actionable context; run lsp_diagnostics on touched files, then `uv run ruff check .`, then the smallest relevant `uv run pytest` subset (include tests/test_ticket_ux.py when UX/output is affected).

instincts.title: Team disband changes require targeted disband tests
instincts.trigger: When changing team shutdown/disband behavior (especially in src/agent_loom/team/core.py) or introducing a new disband pathway
instincts.action: Add/adjust focused tests covering disband semantics in tests/test_team_disband.py; keep teardown deterministic; run uv run pytest tests/test_team_disband.py and uv run ruff check . before calling work done

instincts.title: Treat .opencode as canonical skills store
instincts.trigger: When editing/creating skills and there are multiple skill directories (for example .opencode/skills and .claude/skills)
instincts.action: Only propose skill changes under .opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md and rely on docs/index sync; avoid duplicating or manually maintaining mirror copies elsewhere.

evidence.note: Edits occurred in both .claude/skills and .opencode/skills copies, increasing the importance of enforcing .opencode as canonical.
Recent changes touched both .claude/skills/* and .opencode/skills/*; reinforce that .opencode/skills/* is the canonical location to avoid drift/duplication.
Recent changes touched `.claude/skills/*`, reinforcing the need to treat `.opencode/skills/*` as canonical and avoid duplicating skill sources.
Repo still contains `.claude/skills/*` duplicates that changed alongside `.opencode/skills/*`; strengthens the need to treat `.opencode/skills/*` as canonical and avoid drift/parallel maintenance.

instincts.title: Team ship behavior requires ticket-sync contract test
instincts.trigger: When changing team ship/merge behavior in src/agent_loom/team/core.py, src/agent_loom/team/cli.py, or src/agent_loom/team/merge_queue.py
instincts.action: Add/update a focused contract test in tests/test_team_ship_ticket_sync.py and run `uv run pytest tests/test_team_ship_ticket_sync.py` plus `uv run ruff check .` (after clearing lsp_diagnostics on touched files).

evidence.note: This instinct’s action references lsp_diagnostics; repo standard is `uv run basedpyright`.

instincts.title: Team start/merge config changes require a focused contract test
instincts.trigger: When changing team startup behavior or merge configuration wiring (typically in src/agent_loom/team/core.py and/or src/agent_loom/team/cli.py), especially any logic that selects/validates merge config at team start.
instincts.action: Treat it like a public contract: add/update tests/test_team_start_merge_config.py to lock expected invariants, clear lsp_diagnostics on touched files, then run `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run pytest tests/test_team_start_merge_config.py` before calling work complete.

evidence.note: This instinct’s action references lsp_diagnostics; repo standard is `uv run basedpyright`.

instincts.title: Team prompt changes need section-level contracts
instincts.trigger: When adding or restructuring sections in src/agent_loom/team/prompts.py (or prompt assembly in src/agent_loom/team/core.py).
instincts.action: Make prompt rendering deterministic (explicit ordering, stable headings) and add/expand section-level contract tests in tests/test_team_prompts.py that assert required sections/ordering without relying on nondeterministic data.

evidence.note: Large prompt expansion in src/agent_loom/team/prompts.py was paired with expanded tests/test_team_prompts.py, validating section-level invariants (ordering/headings) as the right abstraction for stability.
Recent change touched src/agent_loom/team/prompts.py and was paired with updates to tests/test_team_prompts.py, reinforcing that prompt assembly needs section-level, deterministic contract coverage.

instincts.title: Team spawn/integrator changes require a contract test
instincts.trigger: When changing team spawn/integrator wiring (typically in src/agent_loom/team/core.py or src/agent_loom/team/cli.py), especially anything that boots an integrator or mediates agent process startup.
instincts.action: Add/update a focused contract test in tests/test_team_spawn_integrator.py that asserts deterministic invariants (what starts, with what args/env, and what is persisted/returned), then run lsp_diagnostics on touched files, uv run ruff check ., and uv run pytest tests/test_team_spawn_integrator.py before calling the work done.

evidence.note: Startup wiring/integrator spawning changes were locked with deterministic invariants in tests/test_team_spawn_integrator.py.

instincts.title: Prefer basedpyright over LSP diagnostics
instincts.trigger: When about to check Python types/diagnostics (or an existing checklist says to run lsp_diagnostics)
instincts.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` and fix findings before `uv run ruff check .` and targeted `uv run pytest ...`.

instincts.title: Keep Compound Opencode Template In Sync
instincts.trigger: When editing Compound plugin/skill/docs behavior that is shipped via a template (for example .opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts or .opencode/skills/*) and the repo contains a scaffold copy under src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.
instincts.action: Update both the repo-root .opencode/* sources and the scaffolded template under src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.opencode/* to keep installation output deterministic; add/adjust tests/test_compound_install.py to lock the expected installed files/contents.

evidence.note: Skill template mirroring stayed consistent by updating both .opencode/skills/... and src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.opencode/skills/..., reducing install drift risk.

Edits touched both canonical and scaffold-mirrored skill copies for workspace-cli-ux-contract-testing, reinforcing the need to keep .opencode/* sources and src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.opencode/* mirrors aligned for deterministic installs.

Recent work updated workflow command docs/skills in both `.opencode/` and the scaffold mirror under `src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.opencode/`, reinforcing that template mirroring must stay in lockstep for deterministic installs.

instincts.title: Compound Install Changes Require Contract Tests
instincts.trigger: When changing src/agent_loom/compound/install.py or src/agent_loom/compound/cli.py (or any behavior that affects generated .opencode/* files).
instincts.action: Update/add assertions in tests/test_compound_install.py for deterministic outputs; then run uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., and uv run pytest tests/test_compound_install.py before calling the work done.

instincts.title: Team Mounts Changes Require Contract Test
instincts.trigger: When editing team mount behavior (notably src/agent_loom/team/core.py) or adding/changing mounts-related logic and outputs.
instincts.action: Lock the behavior with deterministic invariants and update/add coverage in tests/test_team_mounts.py; then run uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., and uv run pytest tests/test_team_mounts.py.

evidence.note: Mount-related behavior evolved with an accompanying update to tests/test_team_mounts.py, validating the 'mounts are a contract' heuristic.
Mount behavior changes were paired with updates to tests/test_team_mounts.py and additional coverage in tests/test_team_spawn_mounts.py.

instincts.title: Merge Queue Changes Require Determinism And Tests
instincts.trigger: When adding/changing merge queue behavior in src/agent_loom/team/merge_queue.py (or wiring it from src/agent_loom/team/core.py).
instincts.action: Make ordering deterministic (explicit sorting/tie-breaks, no set/dict iteration) and add/update focused pytest coverage for the queue semantics; verify with uv run basedpyright then uv run ruff check . then the smallest relevant uv run pytest suite.

instincts.title: Team __init__ exports are a public API
instincts.trigger: When editing src/agent_loom/team/__init__.py exports or adding new team entrypoints
instincts.action: Treat exports as a public contract: keep names stable, avoid side effects on import, and ensure tests cover expected import paths/entrypoints when behavior changes.

instincts.title: Workspace CLI output is a contract
instincts.trigger: When changing user-visible output/flags/formatting in src/agent_loom/workspace/cli.py
instincts.action: Make output deterministic (explicit ordering; no timestamps/randomness/absolute paths). Add/update a focused contract test (prefer tests/test_workspace_cli_ux.py). Verify with: uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli_ux.py.

evidence.note: Recent changes heavily modified src/agent_loom/workspace/cli.py; deterministic CLI UX needs explicit contract coverage.
Recent changes modified user-visible workspace CLI surface in src/agent_loom/workspace/cli.py; reinforces treating output/ordering as a deterministic contract with a focused UX test.

Recent work changed src/agent_loom/workspace/cli.py and adjusted workspace CLI UX contract expectations; reinforces that workspace CLI output/formatting must remain deterministic and should be locked with focused tests.

instincts.title: Workspace core changes require targeted tests
instincts.trigger: When editing src/agent_loom/workspace/core.py, src/agent_loom/workspace/models.py, src/agent_loom/workspace/state.py, src/agent_loom/workspace/repo_ops.py, src/agent_loom/workspace/git_ops.py, src/agent_loom/workspace/poly_ops.py, src/agent_loom/workspace/prime.py, or src/agent_loom/workspace/guards.py
instincts.action: Treat workspace as public API: add/adjust targeted pytest coverage for the behavior/contract being changed (CLI text, model serialization, ordering, guard failures). Run: uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., then the smallest relevant uv run pytest subset (or full suite if unsure).

evidence.note: Recent edits touched workspace core surface area (src/agent_loom/workspace/core.py, guards.py, prime.py, repo_ops.py) alongside multiple focused tests; reinforces targeted-suite expectation.

instincts.title: Core CLI changes require UX contract tests
instincts.trigger: When editing user-visible output/flags/formatting in src/agent_loom/cli.py
instincts.action: Make output deterministic and lock it with a focused pytest contract test (prefer tests/test_cli_ux.py or the existing CLI test module). Verify via: uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., uv run pytest <that test module>.

instincts.title: Sanitize CLI environment/model output
instincts.trigger: When adding CLI output that prints runtime environment or model identifiers (e.g., model id, working directory, platform, dates)
instincts.action: Avoid secrets and machine-specific absolute paths; prefer minimal, stable fields; keep formatting deterministic; add/adjust a contract test asserting stable invariants rather than full dumps.

instincts.title: Init CLI Changes Require UX Contract Test
instincts.trigger: When editing user-visible output/flags/formatting in src/agent_loom/init/cli.py (or init CLI output pathways).
instincts.action: Make output deterministic (stable ordering; no nondeterministic/machine-specific values) and add/update a focused pytest contract test (prefer tests/test_loom_init_cli_ux.py). Verify with: uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., uv run pytest tests/test_loom_init_cli_ux.py.

instincts.title: Core docs are contracts
instincts.trigger: When proposing or making changes to AGENTS.md, LOOM_PROJECT.md, or LOOM_ROADMAP.md (especially deletions or restructures)
instincts.action: Treat these files as contracts: avoid large deletions without replacement; keep always-on blocks short/stable; update AI-managed blocks via CompoundSpec v2 (docs.blocks.upsert + docs.sync); keep paths repo-root-relative; never include secrets or machine-specific absolute paths.

instincts.title: Apply compound spec after JSON-only output
instincts.trigger: You just produced a CompoundSpec v2 JSON payload for learning updates
instincts.action: Immediately call `compound_apply()` next; assume it consumes the prior JSON-only assistant output (no args).
